questions.question:
A control plane component recovery brief is incomplete and contains unsafe scheduler/controller-manager troubleshooting guidance.

A ConfigMap named `component-repair-brief` already exists in namespace `controlplane-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `schedulerManifest` is `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-scheduler.yaml`
- `controllerManagerManifest` is `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml`
- `schedulerHealthz` is `https://127.0.0.1:10259/healthz`
- `controllerManagerHealthz` is `https://127.0.0.1:10257/healthz`
- `schedulerKubeconfig` is `/etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf`
- `controllerManagerKubeconfig` is `/etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf`
- `schedulerLogHint` is `journalctl -u kubelet | grep kube-scheduler`
- `controllerManagerLogHint` is `journalctl -u kubelet | grep kube-controller-manager`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q501/component-repair-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q501/control-plane-checklist.txt` with the sections `Scheduler`, `Controller Manager`, and `Verification`

Do not restart static Pods and do not replace files under `/etc/kubernetes/manifests` during this drill.

verification.description: The repair brief ConfigMap uses the intended manifest paths, kubeconfig references, health endpoints, and log hints
The exported plain-text checklist contains the required sections and exact scheduler/controller-manager troubleshooting commands
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe recovery guidance is removed

questions.question:
A service and pod connectivity diagnostics brief is incomplete and contains stale reachability guidance.

A ConfigMap named `connectivity-brief` already exists in namespace `connectivity-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `debugPod` is `net-debug`
- `serviceName` is `echo-api`
- `servicePort` is `8080`
- `headlessServiceName` is `echo-api-headless`
- `podDnsName` is `echo-api-0.echo-api-headless.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local`
- `serviceProbe` is `kubectl exec -n connectivity-lab net-debug -- wget -qO- http://echo-api:8080/healthz`
- `podProbe` is `kubectl exec -n connectivity-lab net-debug -- wget -qO- http://echo-api-0.echo-api-headless.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local:8080/healthz`
- `dnsProbe` is `kubectl exec -n connectivity-lab net-debug -- nslookup echo-api.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q502/connectivity-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text connectivity matrix to `/tmp/exam/q502/connectivity-matrix.txt` with the sections `Service Path`, `Pod Path`, and `DNS Checks`

Do not change Service selectors, do not patch workloads, and do not create new Pods during this drill.

verification.description: The connectivity brief ConfigMap uses the intended debug Pod, Service names, headless DNS name, and exact probe commands
The exported connectivity matrix contains the required sections and exact service, pod, and DNS checks
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe connectivity guidance is removed

questions.question:
A Service exposure debugging brief is incomplete and contains stale selector and endpoint guidance.

A ConfigMap named `service-exposure-brief` already exists in namespace `service-debug-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `serviceName` is `echo-api`
- `serviceType` is `ClusterIP`
- `selectorKey` is `app`
- `selectorValue` is `echo-api`
- `servicePort` is `8080`
- `targetPort` is `8080`
- `endpointCheck` is `kubectl get endpoints echo-api -n service-debug-lab -o wide`
- `selectorCheck` is `kubectl get svc echo-api -n service-debug-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.app}'`
- `reachabilityCheck` is `kubectl exec -n service-debug-lab net-debug -- wget -qO- http://echo-api:8080/healthz`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q503/service-exposure-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q503/service-exposure-checklist.txt` with the sections `Selector Audit`, `Endpoint Audit`, and `Reachability`

Do not patch Deployments, do not create Ingress or Gateway API resources, and do not delete Services during this drill.

verification.description: The service exposure brief ConfigMap uses the intended Service name, selector contract, ports, and exact endpoint and reachability checks
The exported checklist contains the required sections and exact selector, endpoint, and reachability commands
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe service debugging guidance is removed
